description: Create the Node.js dashboard backend server with REST API endpoints that communicate with the Pumpkin Minecraft server via RCON protocol and read/write the pumpkin.toml configuration file. The backend provides: server status, player list, console command execution, settings management, and system resource monitoring.
steps: Create dashboard/ directory at project root with package.json including dependencies: express, ws (WebSocket for real-time console), toml (for reading/writing pumpkin.toml config), node-rcon or custom RCON client implementation
Create dashboard/src/server.js as the main entry point - Express server listening on port 3000, serving static files from dashboard/public/ and API routes from dashboard/src/routes/

Create dashboard/src/rcon-client.js - a custom RCON client implementation matching the protocol in pumpkin/src/net/rcon/packet.rs. The RCON protocol: 4-byte little-endian length, 4-byte request ID, 4-byte type (3=auth, 2=command), null-terminated body, null padding. Response is similar with type 0=command response, 2=auth response.
Create dashboard/src/routes/status.js - GET /api/status endpoint that returns server online/offline status by attempting RCON connection, plus system resource info (CPU/RAM from os module)
Create dashboard/src/routes/players.js - GET /api/players endpoint that sends 'list' command via RCON to get online players

Create dashboard/src/routes/console.js - POST /api/console endpoint that accepts a command string and sends it via RCON, returning the response. Also set up WebSocket endpoint /ws/console for real-time console streaming

Create dashboard/src/routes/settings.js - GET /api/settings reads pumpkin.toml and returns editable fields (motd, max_players, default_gamemode, default_difficulty). PUT /api/settings writes changes back to pumpkin.toml
Create dashboard/src/routes/power.js - POST /api/power/stop sends 'stop' command via RCON. POST /api/power/restart sends stop then monitors for restart.
Create dashboard/src/config.js - dashboard configuration (RCON host, port, password, pumpkin.toml path) with defaults matching pumpkin-config defaults (localhost:25575)
Create dashboard/src/routes/index.js - aggregates all route modules and exports the Express router
acceptance_criteria: Running 'npm install' in dashboard/ completes without errors
Running 'npm start' in dashboard/ starts the Express server on port 3000
GET /api/status returns JSON with server status fields
GET /api/settings returns JSON with settings from pumpkin.toml (or defaults if file not found)
POST /api/console accepts { command: 'string' } body and returns response
All API endpoints return proper JSON error responses when RCON is unavailable
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/Pumpkin/dashboard && export PATH=/opt/toolchains/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH && timeout 5 npm start || true
